## Preprocess 2026-05-29 HRM data

Read raw QuantStudio output, process melting curves (80–90°C), and save clean CSV.

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(mcmodel)

# raw QuantStudio file
filename = "data-raw/modeling-qPCR/2026-05-29 195141.txt"
all = read_quantstudio(filename)

# plate setting
plate = read.csv(xfun::magic_path("modeling-plate-labels.csv")) |>
  mutate(label_E = ifelse(label_E == 0, 1, ifelse(label_E == 16, 0, 1/2^label_E)),
         label_P = ifelse(label_P == 0, 1, ifelse(label_P == 16, 0, 1/2^label_P)))

sample = plate |> mutate(cycle = 30, rep = 1)

# process melting curves: 80–90°C, step 0.1
mc = quantstudio2mc(all, primer = "V4", plate = sample) |>
  filterData(from = 80, to = 90) |>
  transformData(step = 0.1)

# convert to wide data frame
data = mc_tbl2wider(mc)

# fix experiment date (instrument clock was wrong: shows 2016 instead of 2026)
data = data |> mutate(experiment_date = as.Date("2026-05-29"))

head(data)

# save
write.csv(data, file = "data-clean/20260529.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
